adds.rationale: Clean-energy ETF provides early, diversified exposure to the energy-transition story including utility-scale clean power; a reasonable proxy for the user's nuclear/clean-power 'next wave' bucket given that pure-play nuclear ETFs are not yet liquid as of mid-2022.

news_evidence.summary: EU Parliament votes to label nuclear and gas as 'green' investments in the EU taxonomy, a structural tailwind for clean-energy and nuclear-adjacent funds
Biden administration launches $6B program to rescue at-risk US nuclear reactors, signaling federal support for the existing nuclear fleet

adds.rationale: Dominant AI accelerator supplier with data-center GPU revenue accelerating; recent results and product roadmap (Hopper H100 announced March 2022) make it the clearest single-name proxy for the current AI wave the user explicitly wants to ride.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia unveils Hopper H100 data-center GPU architecture aimed at AI training workloads at GTC 2022
Nvidia Q1 FY23 data-center revenue hits record $3.75B, up 83% year-over-year, despite gaming softness

adds.rationale: Adds a robotics/autonomy leg via Tesla's Optimus humanoid program and FSD development, complementing the BOTZ industrial-robotics ETF with single-name exposure to a likely 'next wave' the user named.

news_evidence.summary: Elon Musk says Tesla aims to have a working Optimus humanoid robot prototype by September 30 AI Day, framing robotics as potentially bigger than the car business
Tesla Q1 2022 deliveries and revenue beat expectations; FSD beta expands user base, supporting the autonomy/robotics thesis

rationale_overall: At mid-2022 the user's starter watchlist has decent AI/general-markets coverage (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, SPY, AGG, BOTZ) and early bets on space (ARKX) and uranium (URA), but lacks a pure AI-accelerator name, a robotics/autonomy single-name, and a clean-energy/nuclear-adjacent ETF (note: pure-play US nuclear ETFs are not yet liquid). Adding NVDA, TSLA, and ICLN fills the three clearest thematic gaps named in the wave thesis while staying under the 12-ticker cap (post-change size = 11) and respecting per-bucket diversity. No removes: the starter set is thin and each existing ticker still maps to a wave bucket the user wants exposure to.
